# BI-260002: Curate archive content for the public site

- Backlog Item ID: BI-260002
- Title: Curate and port selected archive content into the new public site
- Status: Done
- Persona/Actor: Prospective customer or public-site visitor
- Value statement: For a public visitor, deliver the right preserved content from the archive to
  achieve clear product understanding, legal coverage, and add-on discoverability without carrying
  over obsolete behavior.
- Context: The archive contains landing-page content, Add-on marketing, legal, support, and some
  auth/demo/newsletter elements. The new site must selectively keep, drop, or rewrite these.
- Problem statement: Without an explicit curation step, the migration risks copying stale or
  irrelevant content and missing legal/support obligations.
- Expected outcome: The new site contains only the archive-derived public content that still makes
  sense for Crystord, with clear rationale for keep/drop/rework decisions.
- Success metric(s): All curated public content appears in the new site scope; dropped items are
  intentionally absent unless a later backlog item restores them.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - Inventory archive public-facing pages and sections.
  - Map each archive item to Keep, Rework, or Drop.
  - Preserve the Google Add-on page and Add-on legal/support pages.
  - Preserve landing-page product overview, contact path, and YouTube reference.
  - Exclude newsletter, sign-up, and demo flow unless later re-approved.
  - Exclude app-internal authenticated routes from the public-site migration.
- Non-goals: Porting Angular implementation details; rebuilding the SaaS application UI.
- Constraints: The target site remains fully static and GitHub Pages-friendly.
- Dependencies: Review of archive copy for accuracy, especially legal and support content.
- Risks/assumptions: Some archive text may be outdated and require manual rewrite or legal review.
- Validation approach: Manual comparison of curated archive inventory against implemented public
  routes and navigation.
- Priority score: High
- Target window: Before the first public-content implementation milestone
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260002.md
  - ADR: ADR-260002.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - PR/changeset: n/a
